page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the ecs.g5ne family. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external storage solutions and utilizing a KVM hypervisor. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%, alongside a poor single-threaded gzip compression result in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores all positioning in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds for large blocks, though the overall Passmark memory score remains in the bottom 25%.

From a workload perspective, the instance is suited for multi-threaded applications that can leverage its 16 dedicated cores, such as static web serving, Redis database operations, and moderate multi-threaded decompression tasks, which all demonstrate average benchmark throughput. It is less optimal for single-threaded processing or heavy memory-bound operations. The dedicated CPU allocation offers stable performance without resource stealing, representing a standard general-purpose resource profile for enterprise workloads requiring predictable compute without local storage requirements.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Operating on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps, its performance profile shows weak single-core capabilities but average multi-core scaling. Benchmarks place its multi-core CPU performance, static web serving, and Redis operations in the middle 50% tier. This instance is best suited for general-purpose, multi-threaded workloads such as web serving, caching layers, and application servers that require dedicated compute resources but do not depend on high-speed local storage or intensive single-threaded execution.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 7 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering stable multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced general-purpose compute with 7 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance offering balanced multi-core compute and memory for stable, network-dependent enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
7 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor virtualization
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallelized processing tasks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose applications
